links.title: Supraglacial lakes and channels in West Antarctica and Antarctic Peninsula during January 2017 (v.1.1)
Supraglacial lakes and channels in West Antarctica and Antarctic Peninsula during January 2017 (v.1.1)

parquet:columns.name: POLY_AREA
parquet:columns.description: Area of the supraglacial lake polygon as provided in the source vector dataset. This may duplicate or closely correspond to Shape_Area or Area, depending on the source processing workflow.

parquet:columns.name: CENTROID_X
parquet:columns.description: X coordinate of the supraglacial lake polygon centroid. For Antarctic geographic datasets this is typically longitude in decimal degrees, unless the source layer uses a projected coordinate reference system.

parquet:columns.name: CENTROID_Y
parquet:columns.description: Y coordinate of the supraglacial lake polygon centroid. For Antarctic geographic datasets this is typically latitude in decimal degrees, unless the source layer uses a projected coordinate reference system.

parquet:columns.name: Area
parquet:columns.description: Surface area of the mapped supraglacial lake feature. Used to describe lake size and to support derived metrics such as volume estimates and area-perimeter ratios.

parquet:columns.name: Shape_Leng
parquet:columns.description: Perimeter or boundary length of the supraglacial lake polygon geometry.

parquet:columns.name: Shape_Area
parquet:columns.description: Area of the lake polygon geometry calculated by the source GIS software. This may duplicate or closely correspond to POLY_AREA or Area.

parquet:columns.name: Bedrock
parquet:columns.description: Distance from the supraglacial lake feature to the nearest mapped exposed bedrock or rock outcrop, where available.

parquet:columns.name: Coast
parquet:columns.description: Distance from the supraglacial lake feature to the Antarctic coastline or ice-shelf front used in the source analysis.

parquet:columns.name: GL_ON_IS
parquet:columns.description: Flag indicating whether the supraglacial lake is located on an ice shelf or in a grounding-line-related ice-shelf setting, according to the source classification.

parquet:columns.name: Location
parquet:columns.description: Textual location category or regional setting assigned to the supraglacial lake, such as ice sheet, ice shelf, grounding-zone, coastal, or named regional grouping, depending on the source classification.

parquet:columns.name: GL
parquet:columns.description: Distance from the supraglacial lake feature to the Antarctic grounding line used in the source analysis.

parquet:columns.name: Volume
parquet:columns.description: Estimated water volume of the supraglacial lake feature, typically derived from mapped lake area and an empirical or remotely sensed depth relationship.

parquet:columns.name: A_P_Ratio
parquet:columns.description: Ratio of lake polygon area to perimeter, used as a compactness or shape metric for the mapped supraglacial lake.

parquet:columns.name: REMA_Max
parquet:columns.description: Maximum surface elevation from the Reference Elevation Model of Antarctica within or associated with the supraglacial lake polygon.

parquet:columns.name: REMA_Min
parquet:columns.description: Minimum surface elevation from the Reference Elevation Model of Antarctica within or associated with the supraglacial lake polygon.

parquet:columns.name: PolsbyPopp
parquet:columns.description: Shape compactness metric for the lake polygon based on the ratio of polygon area to the area of a circle with the same perimeter. Values closer to 1 indicate a more compact, circular shape.

parquet:columns.name: Fractal
parquet:columns.description: Shape-complexity metric describing the irregularity of the supraglacial lake polygon boundary. Higher values generally indicate a more complex or convoluted outline.

parquet:columns.name: Reock
parquet:columns.description: Shape compactness metric comparing the polygon area with the area of its minimum bounding circle. Values closer to 1 indicate a more compact shape.

parquet:columns.name: Schwartzbe
parquet:columns.description: Shape compactness metric comparing the polygon perimeter with the circumference of a circle of equal area. Values closer to 1 generally indicate a more compact, circular polygon.

parquet:columns.name: LW_Ratio
parquet:columns.description: Ratio of lake length to lake width, used to describe elongation of the supraglacial lake polygon.

parquet:columns.name: Feature_Cl
parquet:columns.description: Classification assigned to the mapped feature in the source dataset. For this asset, features represent supraglacial lakes, but this field may preserve source-level feature class labels or quality classes.

parquet:columns.name: Elevation
parquet:columns.description: Representative surface elevation associated with the supraglacial lake feature, likely derived from REMA or another Antarctic digital elevation model.

parquet:columns.name: Slope
parquet:columns.description: Representative ice-surface slope associated with the supraglacial lake feature.

parquet:columns.name: Speed
parquet:columns.description: Representative ice-flow speed at or near the supraglacial lake feature, derived from an Antarctic ice-velocity product used in the source analysis.

parquet:columns.name: geometry
parquet:columns.description: Polygon geometry representing the mapped supraglacial lake surface extent. Stored as the GeoParquet geometry column.
